From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

2002 mixtape by 50 Cent

This article is about the mixtape by 50 Cent. For other uses, see [Guess Who's Back (disambiguation)](/wiki/Guess_Who%27s_Back_(disambiguation) "Guess Who's Back (disambiguation)").

| Guess Who's Back? | |
| --- | --- |
|  | |
| [Mixtape](/wiki/Mixtape "Mixtape") by [50 Cent](/wiki/50_Cent "50 Cent") | |
| Released | May 21, 2002 |
| Recorded | 2001–2002, Canada |
| [Genre](/wiki/Music_genre "Music genre") | * [Gangsta rap](/wiki/Gangsta_rap "Gangsta rap") * [East Coast hip hop](/wiki/East_Coast_hip_hop "East Coast hip hop") |
| Length | 56:11 |
| [Label](/wiki/Record_label "Record label") | Full Clip |
| [Producer](/wiki/Record_producer "Record producer") | * [Trackmasters](/wiki/Trackmasters "Trackmasters") * Kurt Gowdy * [DJ Clark Kent](/wiki/DJ_Clark_Kent "DJ Clark Kent") * [Father Shaheed](/wiki/Father_Shaheed "Father Shaheed") * [Nottz](/wiki/Nottz "Nottz") * [Sha Money XL](/wiki/Sha_Money_XL "Sha Money XL") * [Red Spyda](/wiki/Red_Spyda "Red Spyda") * Terence Dudley * [Fantom of the Beat](/wiki/Haas_G "Haas G") |
| [50 Cent](/wiki/50_Cent "50 Cent") chronology | |
| |  |  |  | | --- | --- | --- | | *[Power of the Dollar](/wiki/Power_of_the_Dollar "Power of the Dollar")* (2000) | ***Guess Who's Back?*** (2002) | *[50 Cent Is the Future](/wiki/50_Cent_Is_the_Future "50 Cent Is the Future")* (2002) | | |

***Guess Who's Back?*** is the debut official [mixtape](/wiki/Mixtape "Mixtape") by American rapper [50 Cent](/wiki/50_Cent "50 Cent"), released May 21, 2002 on independent label Full Clip Records in the United States. It is his first official release after his 2000 effort *[Power of the Dollar](/wiki/Power_of_the_Dollar "Power of the Dollar")* went unreleased due to [Columbia Records](/wiki/Columbia_Records "Columbia Records")' discovery of a May 2000 shooting where 50 Cent was struck by nine bullets, and was subsequently dropped from the label as a result. The album features production by [Trackmasters](/wiki/Trackmasters "Trackmasters"), [DJ Clark Kent](/wiki/DJ_Clark_Kent "DJ Clark Kent"), [Father Shaheed](/wiki/Father_Shaheed "Father Shaheed"), [Sha Money XL](/wiki/Sha_Money_XL "Sha Money XL"), [Red Spyda](/wiki/Red_Spyda "Red Spyda"), and Terence Dudley. *Guess Who's Back*? received generally positive reviews from music critics and peaked at number 28 on the U.S. [*Billboard* 200](/wiki/Billboard_200 "Billboard 200") chart.[[1]](#cite_note-1)

## Background

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Guess_Who%27s_Back%3F&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Background")]

The mixtape was recorded in [Canada](/wiki/Canada "Canada") after 50 Cent was "[blacklisted](/wiki/Blacklisting "Blacklisting")" in the [recording industry](/wiki/Recording_industry "Recording industry") and was unable to find a studio to work with in the U.S.[[2]](#cite_note-2)

After releasing the mixtape, 50 Cent was discovered by Detroit rapper [Eminem](/wiki/Eminem "Eminem"), who listened to a copy of the album through 50 Cent's attorney, who was working with Eminem's manager, [Paul Rosenberg](/wiki/Paul_Rosenberg_(music_manager) "Paul Rosenberg (music manager)"), and he was signed to [Interscope Records](/wiki/Interscope_Records "Interscope Records").[[3]](#cite_note-3)

The tracks "[Your Life's on the Line](/wiki/Life%27s_on_the_Line "Life's on the Line")", "Corner Bodega", "[Ghetto Qur'an (Forgive Me)](/wiki/Ghetto_Qur%27an_(Forgive_Me) "Ghetto Qur'an (Forgive Me)")" and "As the World Turns" were originally to be released on 50 Cent's shelved album, *[Power of the Dollar](/wiki/Power_of_the_Dollar "Power of the Dollar")*. "Killa Tape" and "That's What's Up" would later appear on the mixtape, *[50 Cent Is the Future](/wiki/50_Cent_Is_the_Future "50 Cent Is the Future")*. "U Not Like Me" and "Life's on the Line" would later appear on 50 Cent's debut studio album, [*Get Rich or Die Tryin'*](/wiki/Get_Rich_or_Die_Tryin%27_(album) "Get Rich or Die Tryin' (album)").
As of May 2004, the album has sold over 400,000 copies in the United States.[[4]](#cite_note-4) The album's cover art was later used in the video for 50 Cent's song, "[Piggy Bank](/wiki/Piggy_Bank_(song) "Piggy Bank (song)")".

## Critical reception

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Guess_Who%27s_Back%3F&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Critical reception")]

Professional ratings

| Review scores | |
| --- | --- |
| Source | Rating |
| [AllMusic](/wiki/AllMusic "AllMusic") | StarStarStarStar[[5]](#cite_note-Birchmeier-5) |
| *[Blender](/wiki/Blender_(magazine) "Blender (magazine)")* | StarStarStarStar[[6]](#cite_note-Weiner-6) |
| [Robert Christgau](/wiki/Robert_Christgau "Robert Christgau") | [(1-star Honorable Mention)](/wiki/Christgau%27s_Consumer_Guide:_Albums_of_the_%2790s#Grading_key "(1-star Honorable Mention)")[[7]](#cite_note-Christgau-7) |
| RapReviews | 8/10[[8]](#cite_note-Juon-8) |
| *[The Rolling Stone Album Guide](/wiki/The_Rolling_Stone_Album_Guide "The Rolling Stone Album Guide")* | StarStarStarHalf star[[9]](#cite_note-Hoard-9) |
| *[Village Voice](/wiki/The_Village_Voice "The Village Voice")* | favorable[[10]](#cite_note-Ex-10) |

Retrospectively, in a 2025 ranking of twenty 50 Cent albums and mixtapes, Al Shipley of *[Spin](/wiki/Spin_(magazine) "Spin (magazine)")* magazine placed *Guess Who's Back?* third, deeming it 50 Cent's "most essential pre-Interscope project" due to its inclusion of some of the best *Power of the Dollar* songs, such as "Corner Bodega" and "Ghetto Qur'an (Forgive Me)".[[11]](#cite_note-11)
